---
title: "nitrix.stats.betareg"
generated: true
signature-hash: e1bbec463cd8d264
description: "Beta regression for proportions in $(0, 1)$."
listing:
  id: nitrix-stats-betareg-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-stats-betareg/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.betareg`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Beta regression for proportions in $(0, 1)$.

Bounded continuous responses -- tissue fractions, fMRI fractional occupancy,
any proportion -- are modelled as
$y \sim \operatorname{Beta}(\mu\phi,\, (1 - \mu)\phi)$ with mean
$\mu = \operatorname{logit}^{-1}(X\beta)$ and a **precision**
$\phi$ (larger $\phi$ = less dispersion).  This is *not* a
standard exponential-family GLM: the score is **digamma**-based, not the
$\mu_\eta^2 / V$ IRLS weight, and the precision is a second parameter
estimated jointly with the mean -- so it gets its own fitter rather than
slotting into a standard GLM core.

The estimator is the Ferrari & Cribari-Neto Fisher-scoring scheme, which
alternates a reweighted-least-squares step for $\beta$ and a Newton step
for $\log\phi$, to a fixed iteration budget (`vmap`-clean over
voxels):

$$
\begin{aligned}
y^{*} &= \operatorname{logit}(y), \quad
\mu^{*} = \psi(\mu\phi) - \psi((1 - \mu)\phi) \\
\nu &= \psi'(\mu\phi) + \psi'((1 - \mu)\phi) \\
W &= \operatorname{diag}\!\left(\phi^2
    (\mathrm{d}\mu / \mathrm{d}\eta)^2 \nu\right), \quad
z = \eta + \frac{y^{*} - \mu^{*}}
    {\phi \, (\mathrm{d}\mu / \mathrm{d}\eta) \, \nu} \\
\beta &\leftarrow (X^{\top} W X)^{-1} X^{\top} W z
\end{aligned}
$$

with the precision score / information

$$
\begin{aligned}
U_\phi &= \sum \mu(y^{*} - \mu^{*}) + \log(1 - y)
    - \psi((1 - \mu)\phi) + \psi(\phi) \\
K_\phi &= \sum \mu^2 \psi'(\mu\phi)
    + (1 - \mu)^2 \psi'((1 - \mu)\phi) - \psi'(\phi),
\end{aligned}
$$

stepped as
$\log\phi \leftarrow \log\phi + U_\phi / (\phi K_\phi)$.  The
fixed-effect covariance is the $\beta$ block of the **joint**
$(\beta, \phi)$ Fisher information inverse (so standard errors account
for estimating $\phi$).  Every solve is a cuSOLVER-free small dense
inverse; $\psi$ (digamma) and its derivatives (polygamma) come from
`jax.scipy.special`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map — voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-stats-betareg-symbols}
:::
